body:
Current `PeerSelectionStrategy` will not provide healthy set of connected peers in FULU fork. It required some changes to better serve our needs in FULU:
- [ ] Auto peer number adjusments according to required subnets. For supernode should work both for super node flag and dynamic validator increase (could be simplified if it's too complex)
- [ ] Tests for auto peer number adjustment
- [ ] Heavily downscore peer that doesn't have subnet subscriptions required with advertised cgc #9773 
- [ ] Adjust scoring of existing peers to avoid dropping peers being an unique source of single subnet
- [ ] Rework scoring of candidate peers to get a healthy set of peers for each subnet. It requires to use existing peers in calculation and, maybe, select known number of candidates. It would be good to use less input and be able to score candidates one by one, but if not, it could be something like 200 peers input, chose 20 appropriate taking existing in scoring model
- [ ] Limit supernode subscriptions, we don't want all peers connected to be supernodes
title: Update PeerSelectionStrategy to fulfill FULU needs

body:
Currently we have peer selection strategy which identifies subnet connections we require (see `Eth2PeerSelectionStrategy` and `SubnetScorer`) and select peers which are subscribed to these subnets to develop good relations on each subscription topic. 

In Fulu especially with dynamic validators custody subnet requirements could vary from 8 to 128 and could jump eventually on already started node when new validators are added. Although there wlll be supernodes providing all 128 subnets, we cannot rely only on supernodes in our peer selection strategy. It would be good to have both common nodes (which usuallly have 8 subnet subscriptions) and supernodes.

It's not clear whether we should mantain some ratio of supernodes/fullnodes in peer selection.
It's not clear if we should do something similar for nodes chosen to fullfil requirements for attnets and sync committee subnets.

Currently we have these flags which could clash with any auto adjustment:
`--p2p-peer-lower-bound`
`--p2p-peer-upper-bound`
`--Xp2p-target-subnet-subscriber-count`
`--p2p-subscribe-all-subnets-enabled`
`--p2p-subscribe-all-custody-subnets-enabled`
Only `--Xp2p-target-subnet-subscriber-count` could be dropped as being experimental, but we must keep lower and upper bound in place, however it's ok to change (increase) their default values and don't seek for a lot peers when not many subnet connections are required (for example).

If we keep everything as is, a lot of operators will need custom peer target values especially after Fulu fork.
title: Automatically adjust number of connected peers in accordance with number of required subnets

## PR Description
Updated Rust KZG library to 0.8.0. Run old benchmarks + added new.

Summary for benhmarks: 
- kzgVerify (single) - RustKZG is 40% slower, precompute doesn't affect both Rust and CKZG
- kzgVerifyBatch - RustKZG is 115% slower, precompute doesn't affect both Rust and CKZG. We doesn't user batch verification in code because we expect gossip arrives not in the same time and it's better to verify it immediately in multi-threaded pool not waiting for arritval of others
- reconstruct 100% dataColumnSidecars from 50%: RustKZG is 11-16% faster, depends on precompute.

Overall I think: 
- we should drop precompute default for supernode. It doesn't benefit us but consumes more memory
- we'd better stay with CKZG

body:
from fulu spec:
```
If a node's custody requirements decrease, it SHOULD NOT update 
the custody_group_count to reflect this reduction. The node SHOULD 
continue to custody and advertise the previous (highest) custody_group_count. 
The node SHOULD continue to respond to any DataColumnSidecar 
request corresponding to the previous (highest) custody_group_count. 
The previous (highest) custody_group_count SHOULD persist across node restarts.
```

#9838 ensures CGC no longer goes down over time, but it remains ephemeral, not surviving restarts, and should be stored in a variable and loaded at startup.

The change that ensures it doesn't reduce is in `DataColumnSidecarCustodyImpl` 
`if (!totalCustodyGroupCount.compareAndSet(oldCustodyGroupCount, newCustodyGroupCount)) {`

it might be elsewhere. but we should also store it in DB if its not already stored.

Potentially it could be managed via `CustodyGroupCountManager` probably makes the most sense. the check in the current location would remain fine, the CGCManager could just control the data it ever sees. 

There appears to be a channel etc, we just need to make sure we're managing the data properly, and loading from storage at startup.
title: Persist CGC change

byRoot RPC should return only non-finalized data, see spec: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/dev/specs/fulu/p2p-interface.md#datacolumnsidecarsbyroot-v1
`Clients MUST support requesting sidecars since minimum_request_epoch, where minimum_request_epoch = max(finalized_epoch, current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). If any root in the request content references a block earlier than minimum_request_epoch, peers MAY respond with error code 3: ResourceUnavailable or not include the data column sidecar in the response.
`

I did it in a bit hacky way.
The right way requires removing of DataColumnReqResp, DataColumnReqRespBatchingImpl, SimpleSidecarRetriever, DataColumnSidecarRetriever, RecoveringSidecarRetriever, SyncPreImportBlockChannel,  DasPreSampler and building all new logic starting from BatchSync.

Other changes include:
- Noisy logging reduced, important logging upgraded from trace to debug
- Fixes in `byRange` handler, `earliestDataColumnSidecarSlot` and `firstIncompleteSlot` are written on finalization and we cannot rely on them

body:
Currently we have 2 steps of cgc set/update:
1. If a node is non-validator node we set cgc to 4 right from the beginning, which could be not true, see #9461. but actually it looks like it's a correct behavior
2. If it's a node with validators, we set it to 0 at the beginning and after first finalization in fulu, we try check if it's filled for the number of effective validators and if YES, we update it.

In order to have stable network, we MUST have cgc set before/on FULU fork, so all network nodes are able to choose right peers for subscriptions to get the right columns. We start subscriptions 2 epochs before the fork so it would be good to have it at least 1 epoch before the fork. Technically as far that there were 0 slots in fulu, it would be true to have any number there so we could fill it in advance, but it will require some changes in the current architecture. 
Start from  `CustodyGroupCountManagerImpl`
title: Set cgc (Custody group count) in advance

## PR Description
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4354/files

- Create `GossipForkSubscriptionsFuluBpo` which overrides `getActivationEpoch` with BPO epoch and sets `ForkInfo` with pre-computed fork digest (that part is hacky)

- Renamed `BlobSchedule` to `BpoForkSchedule`
- Modified `getForkInfo` and `getCurrentForkInfo` in `RecentChainData` to initialise the ForkInfo with computed fork digest for BPO (that part is hacky as well)

Overall, the whole `GossipForkSubscriptions` need to be refactored to pass the fork digest downstream rather than rely on `ForkInfo.getForkDigest(spec)` method. But for the moment the current solution is a small change, aiming to have a devnet working and identify the other current issues.

body:
### Proof of Concept (PoC)
**Specs**: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/tree/dev/specs/fulu

The Fulu fork started on a separate branch as a Proof of Concept (PoC) and was later improved to be closer to a production version, but it is still not good enough to be merged as is.
**Branch**: https://github.com/Nashatyrev/teku/tree/das
**Issues**: https://github.com/Nashatyrev/teku/issues

We will omit most of the finished implementation and leave only the **TODO** items as of today:
- [x] ~~Implement devnet-5 spec Teku PeerDAS~~
- [x] ~~Add Validator Custody https://github.com/Nashatyrev/teku/pull/199~~
- [x] Implement GetBlobsV2 method https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/630
- [x] Shifting cell proof computation to transaction sender (use `kzg_commitments` when it's available from V2 and don't calculate yourself)
- [x] Compliance with distributed blob building spec (non super-node participation added) https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4183
- [x] getBlobsV2 and getPayloadV5 tests
- [x] https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4284
- [x] Full devnet-6 spec compliant https://notes.ethereum.org/@ethpandaops/peerdas-devnet-6
- [x] pass 1.5.0 reference tests
- [x] Full devnet-7 spec compliant https://notes.ethereum.org/@ethpandaops/peerdas-devnet-7

---
### Master implementation

The [current Fulu branch](https://github.com/Nashatyrev/teku/tree/das) will be transferred to the Teku `master` branch incrementally, feature by feature. This is the preliminary list of features it contains. Each feature should be implemented in a separate PR. If a feature is large, it should be split into smaller PRs or sub-features. The list will be updated in the future to reflect all required changes to be transferred and the status of the transfer.


Core:
- [x] SpecConfigFulu + config + presets + config modification cli flags
- [x] Fulu types
- [x] SchemaDefinitionsFulu
- [x] DataColumnSidecars KZG methods API + tests
- [ ] MiscHelpersFulu
- [ ] SpecLogicFulu
- [ ] FuluStateUpgrade

Database:
- [ ] Database store/get/prune Fulu types with key separated like for BlobSidecars

Proposer:
- [ ] Block + DataColumnSidecars production in Fulu
- [ ] Block + DataColumnSidecars publisher Fulu

Networking:
- [ ] Metadata V3 + usage
- [ ] Fulu fork subnet subscriptions update
- [ ] DataColumnPeerSearcher

PeerDAS gossip + RPC:
- [ ] DataColumnSidecar gossip validator
- [ ] RPC DataColumnSidecar by Range message handler
- [ ] RPC DataColumnSidecar by Root message handler
- [ ] RPC DataColumnSidecar by Range API + validator
- [ ] RPC DataColumnSidecar by Root API + validator

PeerDAS main:
- [ ] Implement GetBlobsV2 method https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/630
- [ ] Custody Group Count Manager
- [ ] NodeId + ENR cgc subnet translation manager
- [ ] DataColumnSidecar reconstruction through EL `engine_getBlobsV2`
- [ ] DataColumnSidecar reconstruction on gossip from 50%
- [ ] DataColumnSidecars Custody
- [ ] DataColumnSidecars Sampler (should sample 8 not 4!)
- [ ] Availability checker extension: BlobSidecarsAvailabilityChecker -> BlobSidecar/DataColumnSidecar
- [ ] Forkchoice integration of DataColumnSidecar sampling 

PeerDAS sync:
- [ ] Forward sync in Fulu
- [ ] Backfilling DataColumnSidecars sync in Fulu
- [ ] DataColumnSidecar reconstruction on sync from 50%

Tests:
- [ ] Add more than 9,000 Fulu tests
- [ ] Fulu acceptance tests

Other:
- [ ] BlobSidecar reconstruction for JSON-RPC (see https://github.com/Nashatyrev/teku/pull/102), spec is not finalized, if we keep `getBlobSidecars` we should fill proof with `0x00` or we either introduce `getBlobs`
- [ ] Fulu Metrics
- [ ] Revisit `stream().parallel()` usage, verify whether it leads to any speed gain
- [x] BlobSidecars RPC deprecation https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4286
- [ ] Use byRange RPC requests in syncing as we don't need random sampling any more (we use byRoot historically only)
- [x] BPO forks
- [ ] Maybe address changes in validator custody (stop backfilling but advertise changes after 4096 epochs). It's not MUST but is different from current implementation https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4320
- [x] ~~If spec changes are merged, update RPC by Root in similar manner to byRange, see https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4323~~ (spec changes were not merged, dropping)
